steps.title: Start the division
steps.content: Divide $x^2-3x-6$ by $x-4$. The first quotient term is $x$ because $x^2\div x=x$. Subtract $x(x-4)$ from the numerator.

steps.title: Find the first remainder
steps.content:
After the first subtraction,
$$(x^2-3x-6)-(x^2-4x)=x-6$$
The remaining expression still has degree $1$, so continue the division with one more quotient term.

steps.title: Finish the remainder
steps.content:
The next term is $1$. Subtract $1(x-4)$:
$$(x-6)-(x-4)=-2$$
So the quotient is $x+1$ and the remainder is $-2$.

steps.title: Write the equivalent expression
steps.content: A quotient of $x+1$ with remainder $-2$ gives $$\frac{x^2-3x-6}{x-4}=x+1-\frac{2}{x-4}$$ The answer is D.
